context: Tests whether the agent generates a playbook with correct FQCN module names, verb-first task names, quoted octal permissions, boolean true/false syntax, and idempotent service management via handlers. All criteria map to core skill instructions.

checklist.name: FQCN modules used
checklist.description: Every module call uses its FQCN (e.g., `ansible.builtin.apt`, `ansible.builtin.template`, `ansible.builtin.service`) — no short names like `apt:` or `template:` without the namespace

checklist.name: All tasks have name field
checklist.description: Every task block in the playbook and handlers has a `name:` field — no anonymous tasks

checklist.name: Verb-first task names
checklist.description: Task names begin with an action verb (e.g., 'Install', 'Deploy', 'Ensure', 'Copy', 'Start')

checklist.name: Quoted octal file permissions
checklist.description: Any `mode:` parameter uses a quoted octal string (e.g., `'0644'`, `'0755'`) rather than an unquoted integer

checklist.name: Service restart via handler
checklist.description: The Nginx configuration deployment task uses `notify:` to trigger a handler that restarts/reloads Nginx, rather than a direct `ansible.builtin.service: state: restarted` task

checklist.name: State parameter present
checklist.description: The package installation task and service task both include an explicit `state:` parameter

checklist.name: Playbook header comment
checklist.description: The playbook file starts with a comment block documenting title, description, requirements or variables, and usage command

checklist.name: No shell/command for package install
checklist.description: Nginx installation does NOT use `ansible.builtin.shell` or `ansible.builtin.command` — uses `ansible.builtin.apt` or `ansible.builtin.package`
